# npm-audit allowlist — accepted (triaged) advisories the security gate ignores.
# founder-os#10740 CQ-003: the gate now FAILS on high+critical CVEs. Any high-
# severity advisory whose vulnerable package is listed here (one package name
# per line) is treated as accepted and does NOT fail the gate — so the gate
# flips to high without being blocked by a pre-existing, triaged finding.
#
# RULES: each entry MUST carry a dated reason + a re-review trigger. Keep this
# list minimal — it is an accept-the-risk decision, not a convenience. Prefer
# fixing (bump the dep) over allowlisting whenever a non-breaking fix exists.
#
# Format: a bare package name (matches the npm-audit `.vulnerabilities` key).
# Lines beginning with `#` and blank lines are ignored.

# (empty) — the vite high advisories (#448 triage) are now FIXED by the vite
# ^7.3.3 -> ^7.3.6 bump in this same PR (founder-os#10740 CQ-001 branch),
# fulfilling #448's own re-review trigger ("bump vite in a dedicated dependency
# PR and REMOVE this entry"). The gate now passes on the real fix, not an
# accepted risk. Keep this list empty until a genuinely un-fixable advisory
# requires a triaged, dated exception.
